nodes.label: Task 11: Final verification + knowledge graph refresh
nodes.norm_label: task 11: final verification + knowledge graph refresh

nodes.label: Lazy composition root (no DI framework)
nodes.rationale: The launcher must cold-start fast, so wiring uses plain lazy properties instead of a DI framework — nothing is constructed until the first screen asks for it.

nodes.label: Edge D-pad navigation pattern
nodes.rationale: Zone navigation lives at the activity level: screens consume D-pad events for their own focus first; whatever bubbles up to onKeyDown is an edge press and slides the world to the neighbouring zone. MENU opens the Command Center; BACK and HOME return to center; BACK at home is a no-op because this is a launcher.

nodes.label: Spatial zone grid layout
nodes.rationale: Five zones arranged on a 2D grid (LIVE above, TOOLS left, MEDIA right, AMBIENT below HOME) plus a Command Center overlay. Grid offsets drive slide animations along the axis of travel so moving right genuinely feels like travelling right — the launcher feels like one continuous space rather than a stack of screens.

nodes.label: Widget card system (live info before launch)
nodes.rationale: Each widget turns a repository flow into a WidgetState so apps surface live information before they are opened — the core promise of the card system. Tools zone shows widget cards first and other apps as cards, never an icon grid.
nodes.norm_label: widget card system (live info before launch)

nodes.label: OLED-Dark Glassmorphism Design System
nodes.rationale: True-black base for OLED, frosted glass via layered gradients instead of GPU blur for 60 fps on Shield-class hardware, single cool accent, 10-foot typography, spring-scaled focus. No Material chrome, no ad banners, no dense content rows.

nodes.label: Apps Are Cards, Never Icons
nodes.rationale: Widget cards render live stats so apps are informative before they are ever opened; built-ins register at startup and external plugin APKs can register after discovery.

nodes.label: Demo Repository Pattern
nodes.rationale: Repositories are interfaces with in-memory demo implementations so the launcher is fully navigable out of the box; real providers (M3U/Xtream, Jellyfin/Plex, Open-Meteo, NAS/*arr APIs) swap in behind the same surfaces.

links.source_location: both render clock + weather + current channel from the same flows
